---
title: "Inserting Alt-text & RMarkdown"
output: 
  html_document:
    css: styles.css
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

library(tidyverse)
```

Unfortunately I haven't had success with programmatically adding alt-text with images in Quarto.

## {.tabset}

### HTML 

To view alt-text, open your browser's "Developer Tools" (`Ctrl + Shift + I` in Chrome). Use the pointer and click on the image. You'll be re-directed to the HTML line with the `img` tag and its `fig-alt` attribute.

#### Static images

```{r, eval=FALSE}
Here is an example of a static image with dynamic alt-text:

![](Y:/VISION 2050/Data/Displacement/Displacement Index 2026/docs/Figures/Histograms/01.png){fig-alt="This is a dynamic value: `r nrow(mtcars)`."}
```

![](Y:/VISION 2050/Data/Displacement/Displacement Index 2026/docs/Figures/Histograms/01.png){fig-alt="This is a dynamic value: `r nrow(mtcars)`."}


```{r paths}

img_path <- "Y:/VISION 2050/Data/Displacement/Displacement Index 2026/docs/Figures/Histograms"
img_fnames <- list.files(img_path, full.names = TRUE)[1:3]
img_alttext <- c("hist01", "hist02", "hist03")

```

Use this to dynamically `{r, out.width="100%", fig.alt=img_alttext[1]}` add alt-text in the code chunk settings
```{r, out.width="100%", fig.alt=img_alttext[1]}
knitr::include_graphics(img_fnames[1])
```

#### ggplot2

`labs(alt = )`

`get_alt_text(g)` if the plot is saved as a variable (e.g. `g`)

```{r}
# Create a scatter plot with a linear regression line
g <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) + # Add a linear model fit line
  labs(
    title = "Relationship between Weight and Miles per Gallon",
    x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",
    y = "Miles/(US) gallon",
    caption = "Source: mtcars dataset",
    alt = "A scatterplot showing the relationship between Weight (lbs) and Miles per Gallon."
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r, eval=FALSE, results='asis'}
{r, fig.alt=get_alt_text(g)}

g
```

```{r, fig.alt=get_alt_text(g), echo=FALSE}
g
```



### Resources

- https://www.jumpingrivers.com/blog/accessibility-alt-text-in-r/
